behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: This failure mode is primarily module handoff/wiring (three collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary input), which unit tests can miss. Real OS/network behavior is not required to reproduce this specific bug, so System is not necessary. Agentic is disqualified by the behavior text because it checks inclusion of source data before/at handoff, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run an integration test that wires real collection/orchestration modules with deterministic source adapters returning distinct fixture values for each source, then capture the payload passed into summary generation and assert all three source sections are present and correctly mapped.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering behavior, so Integration is the lowest reliable check beyond unit tests. The behavior text does not require real network/OS conditions; an in-process failing adapter can represent unavailability for this scenario, so System is not required. Agentic is disqualified because the assertion is fallback-note presence and non-abort behavior, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the reporting pipeline with one source adapter that raises an availability error and two normal adapters; execute report generation and assert completion succeeds, the affected section includes `data unavailable`, and the run does not terminate early.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the quality of generated narrative content (factual faithfulness to key values), which a stubbed model cannot validate. This is the deterministic-assertion trap: wrapper logic can pass with mocks while real model output may omit/distort values. Workflow is disqualified by the behavior text because it targets a single summary-generation judgment, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model on a benchmark set of structured inputs with known key values; score outputs for value inclusion/correctness and contradiction/hallucination across repeated runs, enforcing a pass threshold for factual fidelity.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving an externally visible side effect via round-trip readback (message actually arrives in inbox), which triggers the System round-trip rule. Integration tests that only assert send-function calls can give false confidence. Agentic is not required for this specific behavior because the acceptance criterion is delivery success/end-to-end transport, not evaluating LLM decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report job end-to-end through real delivery infrastructure to a test mailbox, then verify via mailbox readback (IMAP/API) that the configured recipient received the message with expected report identifiers/content envelope.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on real infrastructure boundaries: delivery outage handling, filesystem persistence, and cross-run retry side effects. You must observe external effects round-trip (file written, then later delivered) across scheduled executions; in-process mocks can hide timing/persistence defects. Agentic and Workflow are disqualified because no model judgment or multi-agent sequence is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Induce a real delivery outage (e.g., block SMTP/API endpoint), run the scheduled job and verify fallback file creation on disk; restore delivery service, execute the next scheduled run, and verify the persisted report is sent and observable in the recipient inbox.
